site.name: Fitoware
site.tagline: Plant passport and traceability for nurseries
site.desc: Software for nurseries and ornamental plants: botanical species catalogue and issuance of the European Plant Passport (EU Regulation 2016/2031) as a PDF with A·B·C·D elements and a QR code.
hero.subtitle: Fitoware manages your botanical species catalogue, links it to your items and issues the European Plant Passport as a PDF with the A·B·C·D elements and a QR code, ready to print the nursery label.
hero.audience: Nurseries · Ornamental plants · Agriculture · Plant trade
stats.title: The four passport elements, automated
features.title: Everything a nursery needs to comply
features.subtitle: From the species catalogue to the printed label, without leaving your management software.
feat.species.d: Botanical name, genus, family and species code. Each species links to one or more items.
feat.passport.d: PDF with the A·B·C·D elements, QR code and the official 2017/2313 format, from the delivery note or item.
feat.traceability.d: From the source batch to the final label. Traceability code (element C) on every passport.
feat.labels.d: Automatic EAN-13 code generation and label printing with barcode and traceability QR code.
feat.pz.d: PZ indicator and quarantine organism for species that require it in a protected zone.
feat.duplicate.d: Print duplicate passports on A4, two per sheet, to file and hand over.
how.title: From adding the species to the label in three steps
how.s1.d: Enter the botanical name and mark it as "requires passport". It links to the item.
how.s2.t: Sell or prepare the delivery note
how.s2.d: You work as usual. Fitoware detects the items that need a passport.
how.s3.d: Generate the PDF with A·B·C·D and QR, or the barcode label, ready to stick on.
editions.title: Choose where your plant passport lives
editions.subtitle: They cover the same regulation —the European Plant Passport—. What changes is the platform it runs on.
editions.regulation: Both editions comply with EU Regulation 2016/2031 and the official 2017/2313 format.
ed.bc.name: Fitoware for Business Central
ed.bc.d: A native AL extension for companies already running on Business Central. It integrates with Microsoft's standard items, warehouses and sales. Implementation and support by our partner IONNAVIS.
ed.ge.name: Fitoware for GestioERP
ed.ge.d: A module inside GestioERP: the fast, affordable route for nurseries that need the plant passport without a corporate ERP project.
group.title: A product by Slave Computers, S.L.
group.desc: Fitoware is a product by Slave Computers, S.L., part of the SIAM Cloud group: over 15 years building cloud business software from Botarell.
cta.title: Does your nursery need the plant passport?
cta.subtitle: Tell us how you work and we'll show you the edition that fits best: Business Central or GestioERP.

legal.title: Legal notice
legal.p1: This website (fitoware.com) is owned by Slave Computers, S.L., a company within the SIAM Cloud group. Fitoware is a trademark of Slave Computers, S.L.
legal.p2: The site's purpose is to provide information about the Fitoware software for managing nurseries, ornamental plants and issuing the European Plant Passport.
legal.p3: The contents, trademarks and logos are protected by intellectual and industrial property rights. Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central is a trademark of Microsoft. GestioERP is a trademark of the SIAM Cloud group.
legal.p4: Data provided through the contact form is processed for the sole purpose of handling your request. You may exercise your rights of access, rectification and erasure by writing to the contact address.

cookies.banner.text: We use our own technical cookies for the site to work and, with your permission, measurement cookies.
cookies.title: Cookie policy
cookies.intro: This policy explains what cookies are, which ones fitoware.com uses and how you can manage them.
cookies.p1: A cookie is a small text file that a website stores in your browser. It is used to remember information about your visit, such as your language or preferences.
cookies.p2: We use technical cookies necessary for the site to work (for example, to remember your language choice and your cookie consent). If you accept, we may use anonymous measurement cookies to understand how the site is used.
cookies.p3: You can accept or reject non-essential cookies from the notice shown when you enter. You can also block or delete cookies from your browser settings at any time.
cookies.p4: We may update this policy to reflect technical or legal changes. We recommend reviewing it periodically.

star.title: The flagship software of Slave Computers
star.desc: Fitoware is the in-house product that most nurseries and ornamental-plant growers rely on every day to comply with the European plant passport without the hassle.
why.title: Comply with the regulation without slowing the nursery down
why.b1.d: Issue the European Plant Passport with the official 2017/2313 format and all its elements — no Word templates or manual calculations.
why.b2.d: The passport is generated from the delivery note or invoice you already make. No duplicated work in another program.
why.b3.d: Each species is linked to the item and each batch is traced through to the final label, with a QR code.
why.b4.d: For small businesses on GestioERP, or for larger ones already on Microsoft Dynamics 365 with support from partner IONNAVIS.
reg.title: European Plant Passport
reg.desc: Since 2019, Regulation (EU) 2016/2031 requires most plants intended for planting to circulate with a plant passport. Fitoware generates it in the harmonised 2017/2313 format.
reg.b1: Element A: botanical name of the species or taxon.
reg.b3: Element C: traceability code · Element D: country of origin.
audience.title: Built for the green sector
compare.title: Which edition is right for me?
compare.subtitle: Both comply with the same regulation; the platform and the type of company change.
faq.title: Common questions about the passport
faq.q1: What is the European Plant Passport?
faq.a1: It is the official document certifying that a plant meets EU health requirements to circulate within the territory. It is governed by Regulation (EU) 2016/2031 and its format, 2017/2313.
faq.q2: Is my nursery required to issue it?
faq.a2: In general, any professional operator selling plants for planting must issue the passport. Fitoware lets you mark which species require it and automates its issuance.
faq.q3: What is the difference between the two editions?
faq.a3: The regulation is the same. The GestioERP edition is the fast, affordable route for small businesses; the Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central edition is for companies already on that ERP, with support from partner IONNAVIS.
faq.q4: Do I already need an ERP to use Fitoware?
faq.a4: No. With the GestioERP edition you get the ERP and the passport in one package, ready to start without a corporate project.
faq.a5: It is the Official Register number for plant Producers, Traders and Importers that identifies the professional operator. It forms element B of the passport and is set once.
figs.title: Fitoware in four figures
checklist.title: From the botanical catalogue to the printed label
checklist.subtitle: Every detail of the European Plant Passport, covered out of the box.
ck.1: Botanical name: genus, species, family and code
ck.2: Link the species to one or more items
ck.8: Traceability QR code on the label
ck.12: A4 duplicate (2-up) to file and hand over
cultivate.eyebrow: We grow software for the green sector
cultivate.title: Made by and for nurseries
cultivate.desc: Fitoware comes from real work with nurseries and ornamental-plant growers. It is not a generic passport add-on: it is built from day one for the green sector, with botanical vocabulary, a nursery's workflows and phytosanitary regulation at its core.
cultivate.p1.d: Genus, species, family, common name and code — no forced generic fields.
cultivate.p2.d: From adding the species to the sale and the label, with no artificial steps.
cultivate.p3.d: Official 2017/2313 format and the four passport elements, maintained by us.
support.title: Remote support and maintenance from our expert technicians
support.desc: An exclusive service for Fitoware clients with a maximum response time of 4 hours for emergencies, dedicated email and phone, and on-site assistance if it cannot be solved remotely. No minimum contract period: you only pay if you are happy with the service.
